name: README
headers: IBM QUOTING ASSISTANT — DATA SOURCE OF TRUTH
rows: The single source of truth for the IBM Quoting Assistant (BOB). All part numbers, prices, licensing
rates, and discovery questions live here — NOT hard-coded in the prompt. To update the tool, edit this
file. BOB reads it; the prompt just tells BOB how to use it.
1. User picks a product (Verify / NS1 / Vault).
2. BOB reads the Discovery_Questions tab for that product and asks them.
3. BOB computes units using the rate tables (Verify_RU_Rates, Vault_RU_Counting, NS1_Pricing_Tiers).
4. BOB returns part numbers + quantities from the *_Parts tabs, plus a ballpark LIST price.
• Never invent a part number. If a cell says PENDING, the tool must say the part number isn't available yet.
• NS1 part numbers are PENDING — source from CPQ / Tony Nicolakis / Nick Lammert. NS1 prices are ILLUSTRATIVE.
• All prices are LIST. Real pricing, discounts (Optimal Pricing/OPG) and approvals happen in CPQ.
• Vault: the Platform/RU model and the Client/RVU model may NOT be mixed for one customer.
Source documents: Verify SaaS RU calculator (xlsx) · NS1 L2 Seller Deck (PDF) · IBM HashiCorp Product
Pricing Guidance (PDF, dated June 18 2026). Validated: Verify 500-users-SSO example = 50 RU of D0231ZX.
